body: PT Aneka Tambang Tbk (ANTM) has launched a special edition thematic gold to celebrate the upcoming 2025 Chinese New Year, offering two variants of gold bars weighing 8 grams and 88 grams, as well as a gift series, with advanced security features and exclusive packaging. The product, available through Antam's Logam Mulia gold boutiques and its official website since January 9, 2025, is expected to be a high-value long-term investment instrument, capitalizing on the symbolic significance of the Year of the Wood Snake in Chinese culture.
title: Aneka Tambang Launches Special Edition Gold Bars to Celebrate 2025 Chinese New Year

body: PT Multi Spunindo Jaya Tbk (MSJA) saw its stock price increase by 12.66% to Rp338 per share from its IPO price of Rp300 per share, raising Rp264.70 billion in its IPO, with proceeds allocated to capital expenditures, working capital, and repayment of bank liabilities. In contrast, PT Sinergi Multi Lestariindo Tbk (SMLE) experienced a 34.85% decline in its stock price to Rp114 per share from its IPO price of Rp175, with proceeds from its IPO allocated to purchasing a warehouse, developing a research laboratory, and working capital.
title: Two Indonesian Companies Show Contrasting Stock Performance One Year After IPO

body: PT Bliss Properti Indonesia Tbk (POSA) has increased the paid-up capital of its three subsidiaries, PT Tanjung Pinang Indah, PT Lahan Makmur Indah, and PT ABC Bali Laut, through debt-to-equity conversion, resulting in Posa holding a significant stake in each subsidiary, with a total nominal value of IDR 398.72 billion. This capital increase does not impact Posa's operations, legal standing, financial condition, or business continuity, demonstrating the company's commitment to strategically strengthening its capital structure without disrupting operational stability.
title: PT Bliss Properti Indonesia increases paid-up capital of three subsidiaries through debt-to-equity conversion mechanism.

body: PT Amman Mineral Internasional TBK (AMMN) reported a 116.78% year-on-year revenue growth to USD 2.5 billion, driven by significant increases in copper and gold sales, with net profit surging 958% to USD 719.7 million, resulting in a net profit margin increase from 6% to nearly 29%. Despite the strong financial performance, AMMN's stock has dropped 25.55% over the past six months, but has experienced a 25.46% increase over the past year, with a slight 2.11% edge up since the market opened today.
title: PT Amman Mineral Internasional reports exponential growth in revenue and net profit in Q3 2024.

body: Investors increased their stakes in several Indonesian companies, including PT Nusantara Pelabuhan Handal TBK (24.15%), PT Sarimelati Kencana TBK (3.88 million shares), PT Jaya Trishindo TBK (1 million shares), PT Wintermar Offshore Marine TBK (950,000 shares), and PT Panorama Sentrawisata TBK (900,000 shares). Meanwhile, overseas investors sold shares in companies such as PT Bumi Resources TBK (12.81 million shares), PT Sumber Mas Konstruksi TBK (4.13 million shares), PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur TBK (10.13 million shares), and PT Fast Food Indonesia TBK (nearly 650,000 shares).
title: Investors Make Moves in Indonesian Market with Notable Transactions in Various Sectors

body: PT Indonesian Paradise Property Tbk (INPP) has divested 36.7% of PT Narpati Arjaya Properti to Hankyu Hanshin Properties ID-BWC LLC, Japan, securing IDR 652.65 billion in proceeds, which will be allocated towards working capital and business expansion. Following the transaction, INPP's stake in Narpati Arjaya Properti has decreased to 55.22%, with the company remaining optimistic about boosting its performance in the property industry.
title: Indonesian Paradise Property divests 36.7% stake in PT Narpati Arjaya Properti to Japan's Hankyu Hanshin Properties for IDR 652.65 billion.

body: PT Bank Syariah Indonesia Tbk (BSI) recorded a transaction volume of IDR 85 trillion in government Sharia securities from January to November 2024, marking a 65.38% growth, driven by strong collaboration with customers and the Ministry of Finance. The bank distributed five SBSN series, all of which exceeded targets set by the Ministry of Finance, with the highest achievement being 275% of the target for the ST013 series.
title: BSI Records 65.38% Growth in Government Sharia Securities Transactions, Reaching IDR 85 Trillion.

body: PT Cashlez Worldwide Indonesia Tbk has appointed Willy Chandry as its new President Director, replacing Irianto Kusumadjaja, with the goal of boosting the company's performance amidst fierce competition in the payment service industry. Chandry, with his extensive experience in the financial industry, is expected to bring strategic leadership to the company, driving optimism for better results in the future.
title: PT Cashlez Worldwide Indonesia Appoints Willy Chandry as New President Director to Boost Company Performance.

body: The Indonesian Composite Stock Price Index fell 0.22% to 7,064.59 on Thursday, with a total trading value of IDR 7.86 trillion (USD 484 million) and a volume of 16.25 billion shares. PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BCA) led the gainers with a 1.81% increase, while PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk (BRE) was the top loser with a 2.39% decline, and foreign investors recorded a net buy of IDR 38.84 billion shares.
title: Jakarta Composite Stock Price Index Falls 0.22% Amid Slight Correction

body: Foreign investors net sold 456 million shares on Thursday, January 9, with PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) experiencing the largest net sell of 745.71 million shares, followed by PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) with 36.59 million shares. On the other hand, PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) saw the highest net buy of 117.96 million shares, followed by PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) with 51.39 million shares.
title: Foreign investors in Jakarta's stock market sell 456 million shares, with PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK experiencing the largest net sell.

body: PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) and PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) secured the top two positions in net foreign buy transactions, with BUMI recording a net foreign buy of 117.96 million shares and BRMS posting a net foreign buy of 51.39 million shares. At the close of trading, BUMI's stock price settled at IDR 108 per share, with a total of 998.46 million shares traded, while BRMS closed at IDR 382 per share, with a total of 687.95 million shares traded.
title: Bumi Resources and Bumi Resources Minerals top net foreign buy transactions in Thursday's stock trading.

body: PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk (BNI) has disbursed IDR 900 billion in loans to over 48,000 Indonesian migrant workers through its People's Business Credit program, with IDR 25 billion disbursed in 2024 alone at an interest rate of 6% per annum. By the end of 2024, BNI aims to optimize its loan distribution to migrant workers, positioning it as a primary source of pre-departure financing, and promote growth of small and medium enterprises through business matching programs.
title: BNI reaffirms commitment to supporting Indonesian migrant workers through financing access and education programs.

body: PT Raharja Energi Cepu, a subsidiary of PT Rukun Raharja Tbk (RAJA), has secured a USD 6.90 million cash-on-call facility to develop the Cepu block in East Java, with an interest rate of SOFR+5% or 10.04%. The facility aims to support the company's business development in the upstream energy industry and boost future profits.
title: PT Raharja Energi Cepu secures USD 6.90 million cash-on-call facility for Cepu block development in East Java.

body: PT Elang Mahkota Teknologi Tbk (Elang) acquired 36.22 million shares, while DBS Bank Ltd (DBS) increased its stake in PT Sarimelati Kencana Tbk to 8% by purchasing 32 million shares. Meanwhile, HSBC-Fund SVS A/C Chengdong Investment Corp-Self sold 50 million shares of PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI), resulting in a 10.52% decrease in shares.
title: Multiple foreign and local investors engage in significant share purchases and sales in various Indonesian companies.

body: PT Hero Global Investment Tbk (HGII) aims to triple its revenue to IDR 545.8 billion and net profit to IDR 95 billion by 2025, driven by contributions from its three existing projects, including two mini hydro power plants and a biogas power plant. The company also plans to expand its operations with two new projects in North Sumatra, including a 25 MW hydropower plant and a 10 MW mini hydro power plant, which are expected to commence commercial operations in 2028.
title: PT Hero Global Investment targets threefold revenue and net profit increase by 2025 driven by existing and new renewable energy projects.

body: Indonesia's foreign exchange reserves increased by 3.66% to USD 155.7 billion in December 2024, driven by tax service revenue, government foreign loan withdrawals, and oil and gas income. The reserves, equivalent to 6.7 months of imports or 6.5 months of government foreign debt payments, are well above the international adequacy standard of 3 months of imports, enhancing external sector resilience and macroeconomic stability.
title: Indonesia's foreign exchange reserves increase to USD 155.7 billion in December 2024, driven by tax revenue and foreign loan withdrawals.

body: PT Raja Roti Cemerlang TBK (BRRC) plans to export its bread crumb products to Australia, securing a business partner in Perth, and targets revenues of Rp150 billion this year with a projected net profit margin of 5%. The company will also expand its marketing efforts in Indonesia, adding three new production facilities, and launch new products, including "bubble crumbs", to capitalize on growing demand from the food and beverage industry.
title: PT Raja Roti Cemerlang to Export Bread Crumb Products to Australia and Expand Domestic Operations

body: PT Raja Roti Cemerlang TBK (BRRC) debuted on the Indonesia Stock Exchange, with its shares rising 18.1% to IDR 248 per share, after raising IDR 61.22 billion in fresh capital through its initial public offering (IPO) of 291.5 million shares at IDR 210 per share. The company also issued 145.75 million series I warrants, which could potentially raise an additional IDR 30.60 billion, with all funds to be used for working capital, including increasing raw material stock and covering operational expenses.
title: PT Raja Roti Cemerlang TBK makes successful debut on Indonesia Stock Exchange with 18.1% share price increase.

body: PT Hero Global Investment Tbk (HGII) debuted on the Indonesia Stock Exchange, with its shares rising 22% to IDR 250 per share from the initial offering price of IDR 200 per share, raising IDR 260 billion in fresh funds. The IPO proceeds will be used to expand renewable energy generation capacity, with a target of 100 MW by 2031, and Shikoku Electric Power Company, Inc. will acquire a 25% stake in HGII through its subsidiary SEP International Netherlands B.V.
title: PT Hero Global Investment debuts on Indonesia Stock Exchange with 22% share price surge after raising IDR 260 billion in IPO funds for renewable energy expansion.

body: Yulisar Khiat, a member of the board of directors at PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk (HEAL), increased his shareholding by purchasing 600 million shares at IDR 1,580 per share, totaling IDR 948 million, raising his stake to 12.765% of the company's 15.36 billion outstanding shares. Following the transaction, Khiat's ownership increased to 1.961 billion shares from 1.960 billion shares, with other major shareholders including PT Astra International Tbk (ASII) with a 7.32% stake and public shareholders holding 59.15%.
title: Yulisar Khiat, Director of PT Medikaloka Hermina, Increases Shareholding with Purchase of 600 Million Shares.

body: PT Sepatu Bata Tbk (BATA) has signed a trademark license agreement with Bata Brand S.A., which will erase the company's debts from 2022-2024, amounting to over USD 4.8 million, to aid its financial performance. The debt write-off is crucial for Bata, which has been struggling with consecutive losses since 2022, with net losses surging 138% year-on-year in December 2023 and 151% year-on-year in September 2024, and its assets and equity also declining significantly.
title: Bata signs trademark license agreement to erase over USD 4.8 million in debts from 2022-2024.

body: PT Adhi Karta Pratama Tbk (NICE) saw its stock price increase by 2.25% to IDR 364, but has corrected 18.72% since its IPO in January 2024, with its current closing price of IDR 356 significantly lower than its IPO price of IDR 438 per share. The company raised IDR 532.78 billion from its IPO, with a shareholder structure comprising PT Energy Battery (60%), PT Sunag Mas Minerals (10.43%), PT Inti Mega Ventura (9.57%), and public shareholders (20%).
title: PT Adhi Kartiko Pratama's stock price corrects 18.72% since IPO debut despite slight increase in today's trading session.

body: PT Sumber Energi Andalan TBK (ITMA) established four new subsidiaries, PT Ambara Bahari Nusantara, PT Askara Energi Andalan, PT Begawan Energi Sejati, and PT Paramita Bahari Andalan, with an authorized capital of IDR 1 million per share and issued paid-up capital of IDR 600 million per share, to handle freight transportation and leasing activities. The shareholder structure is standardized, with Bipi holding 10% shares and APT controlling the remaining 90% in each subsidiary.
title: PT Sumber Energi Andalan establishes four new subsidiaries to handle freight transportation and leasing activities.

body: PT Suryalaya Anindita International (SSIA) has signed a credit agreement with PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) for IDR 1.4 trillion, with a 10-year term, to fund the renovation of Melia Bali Hotels & Resorts and extension of land utilization. The loan facility is expected to increase the company's consolidated revenue, but will also add interest expenses and depreciation from the renovation, with no operating income from the hotel expected in 2025.
title: PT Suryalaya Anindita International secures IDR 1.4 trillion credit facility from PT Bank Central Asia for hotel renovation and land utilization.

body: PT Petrindo Jaya Kreasi TBK (CUAN) and PT Tanah Laut TBK (INDX) are being closely monitored by the Indonesia Stock Exchange authorities due to unusual market activity, with CUAN's stock price surging 12.19% to IDR 13,800 per share and INDX's price jumping 17.43% to IDR 128. The authorities are cautioning investors to carefully review the companies' performance, corporate actions, and responses before making investment decisions.
title: Indonesian Stock Exchange Authorities Monitor Two Companies After Unusual Market Activity

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) closed 0.04% weaker at 7,080.35 on Wednesday, with a transaction volume of 16.13 billion shares and a transaction value of IDR 9.38 trillion. PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI) led the gainers with a 2.68% increase, while PT Amman Mineral Internasional Tbk (AMIN) was the top laggard with a 4.60% decline, as foreign investors continued their net-sell trend, with PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) being the most sold shares.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Closes 0.04% Lower at 7,080.35 Amid Mixed Market Performance

body: PT Habco Trans Maritima Tbk (HATM) has established a joint venture, PT Habco Segara Crewing, with Hong Kong-based Seacon Crew Management Co. Ltd, with HATM holding 51% of the IDR10 billion paid-up capital, to provide crew recruitment and placement management services for Indonesian and foreign-flagged vessels.
title: PT Habco Trans Maritima forms joint venture with Seacon Crew Management to provide crew recruitment and placement services.

body: Foreign investors sold a net 1.16 billion shares on Wednesday, with PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) being the top seller with 527.19 million shares, followed by PT DMS Propertindo Tbk (DMS) with 210.68 million shares. On the other hand, PT Barito Pacific Tbk (BRPT) saw the largest net buy of 18.77 million shares, followed by PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk (BULL) with 16.16 million shares.
title: Foreign investors in Indonesia's stock market sell more shares than they buy, with a net sell position of 1.16 billion shares.

body: PT Bukit Asam Tbk (PTBA) reported that its exploration activities in the fourth quarter, conducted in collaboration with Sucofindo, cost RP21.69 billion and resulted in 59 drill holes totaling 11,204 meters, while its subsidiary PT Internasional Prima Coal's exploration activities cost RP2.4 billion and covered 2,248 meters. The company allocated additional funds for database management, software licensing, and other operational activities, with plans to continue exploration in Tanjung Enim until 2025.
title: PT Bukit Asam reports on its fourth-quarter exploration activities in Tanjung Enim area and IPC area.

body: PT Asuransi Digital Bersama TBK (YOII) raised Rp41.2 billion through its initial public offering, with its shares surging 29% to Rp129 per share on its first trading day. The company plans to allocate around 20% of the IPO funds to develop its "insurance wallet" digital application, which aims to make insurance services more accessible and inclusive.
title: PT Asuransi Digital Bersama TBK Lists Shares on Indonesia Stock Exchange, Raises Rp41.2 Billion Through IPO
